articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047: Green Growth & Digital Leap' Blueprint
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the ambitious 'Vision Bharat 2047: Green Growth & Digital Leap' blueprint, outlining India's strategic path towards sustainable development and technological leadership by its centenary of independence.
• The vision emphasizes a multi-pronged approach, focusing on significant investments in renewable energy, green hydrogen, sustainable infrastructure, and advanced digital public infrastructure.
• Key pillars include achieving net-zero emissions targets, expanding digital literacy and connectivity to every village, fostering indigenous innovation in AI and quantum computing, and positioning India as a global manufacturing hub for green technologies.
• The blueprint integrates economic growth with environmental protection, aiming for a circular economy model and significant improvements in resource efficiency across all sectors.
• It proposes a robust framework for public-private partnerships and international collaborations to mobilize the necessary capital and expertise for these transformative initiatives.

Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

Critically examine how the blueprint addresses issues of resource mobilization and inclusive growth.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting India's tricolour merging with elements of green energy and digital circuits, symbolizing 'Vision Bharat 2047'.
articles.trivia: India aims to achieve Net-Zero emissions by 2070, a target that 'Vision Bharat 2047' significantly contributes to.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Begins Review of Electoral Bonds Scheme after Centre's Affidavit
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today commenced a crucial review of the Electoral Bonds Scheme, following a detailed affidavit submitted by the Union Government defending its transparency and constitutional validity.
• Petitioners have argued that the scheme promotes opacity in political funding and violates the 'right to know' of citizens, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The Centre's affidavit highlights the scheme's role in curbing black money in elections and providing a cleaner, digital channel for political donations, ensuring donor anonymity against harassment.
• The court is expected to hear arguments on various aspects, including the impact on free and fair elections, the balance between donor privacy and public transparency, and the implications for corporate funding.
• This review comes amidst ongoing public debate regarding electoral finance reforms and the need for greater accountability in political funding.

Parliament and State Legislatures—structure, functioning, conduct of business, powers & privileges and issues arising out of these.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.

mainsPoints: Analyze the arguments for and against the Electoral Bonds Scheme in the context of electoral transparency and political funding.
Discuss the constitutional implications of the scheme, particularly concerning the 'right to know' and free and fair elections.
Suggest alternative reforms for political funding that balance transparency with practical considerations.
Critically evaluate the Supreme Court's potential role in shaping electoral finance laws in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel and legal books, symbolizing the Supreme Court's judicial review.
articles.trivia: The concept of 'electoral trusts' was introduced in India in 2013 to bring more transparency to political funding, preceding electoral bonds.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 'Sustainable Finance Working Group' Meeting in New Delhi
articles.explainer:
• India today hosted the first 'Sustainable Finance Working Group' (SFWG) meeting of the G20 in New Delhi, focusing on mobilizing finance for climate action and sustainable development goals.
• Delegates from G20 member countries, invited guest countries, and international organizations participated, discussing strategies to enhance the flow of capital towards green and resilient infrastructure.
• Key agenda items included developing common taxonomies for sustainable finance, promoting blended finance mechanisms, and addressing debt sustainability challenges in developing nations.
• India's presidency emphasizes a 'human-centric' approach to global challenges, advocating for equitable access to climate finance and technology transfers.
• The outcomes of this meeting are expected to inform the broader G20 agenda on climate change and economic resilience for the year.

Discuss the significance of developing common taxonomies for sustainable finance and the challenges in achieving global consensus.

articles.imageDescription: A G20 logo with a backdrop of the Indian Parliament, symbolizing India's leadership.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis as a forum for finance ministers and central bank governors.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Advanced Test Flight of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of an advanced test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator, marking a significant step towards cost-effective space access.
• The experimental flight involved a precise landing maneuver at an autonomous runway, demonstrating crucial capabilities for future orbital re-entry and landing of space vehicles.
• This test validates several critical technologies, including autonomous navigation, guidance and control, thermal protection systems, and re-entry trajectory management, essential for developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
• ISRO aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making satellite deployment and human spaceflight more economically viable through RLV technology.
• The success reinforces India's position in the global space race and its commitment to indigenous technological advancements in space exploration.

Analyze the key technological challenges involved in developing a fully reusable launch vehicle and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the potential economic and geopolitical advantages India stands to gain from mastering RLV technology.
Examine how this achievement aligns with India's broader vision for self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in critical technologies.

articles.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested the RLV-TD in a sub-orbital mission in 2016, demonstrating its hypersonic flight and re-entry capabilities.

articles.headline: Revised National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) 2026-2030 Unveiled
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched the revised National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) for the period 2026-2030, aligning with India's enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• The updated plan incorporates new missions focusing on climate-resilient agriculture, urban sustainability, blue economy, and enhanced public participation in climate mitigation and adaptation efforts.
• It sets ambitious targets for increasing renewable energy capacity, improving energy efficiency, and promoting sustainable transportation and industrial practices.
• A key feature is the emphasis on leveraging digital technologies and data analytics for climate monitoring, early warning systems, and impact assessment across various sectors.
• The revised NAPCC aims to strengthen India's commitment to global climate goals while ensuring equitable and inclusive development domestically.

Disaster and disaster management.

Discuss the significance of integrating new missions like climate-resilient agriculture and blue economy into the national climate plan.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in leveraging digital technologies for effective climate monitoring and adaptation in India.
Examine the role of public participation and multi-stakeholder engagement in achieving the targets set by the revised NAPCC.
articles.imageDescription: A visual representation of interconnected ecosystems and renewable energy sources, symbolizing the comprehensive nature of NAPCC.
articles.trivia: The original NAPCC was launched in 2008, outlining eight national missions to address climate change.

articles.headline: RBI Releases Half-Yearly Financial Stability Report: Highlights Resilient Banking Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released its latest half-yearly Financial Stability Report (FSR), indicating a resilient Indian banking sector amidst evolving global and domestic economic conditions.
• The report noted a continued improvement in asset quality for both public and private sector banks, with Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) showing a downward trend.
• It highlighted the robust capital adequacy ratios across the banking system, providing a strong buffer against potential shocks.
• However, the FSR cautioned about emerging risks from global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and the increasing complexity of cybersecurity threats to the financial system.
• The report emphasized the need for continuous vigilance, prudent risk management, and further strengthening of regulatory frameworks to maintain financial stability.

Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development in Phase III Rollout
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today announced the commencement of Phase III of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, with a significant emphasis on integrating vocational training and skill development across all levels of education.
• This phase aims to bridge the skill gap in the workforce by introducing vocational courses from middle school onwards, offering multiple entry and exit points, and ensuring industry alignment of curricula.
• Key initiatives include establishing more skill hubs, forging partnerships with industries for apprenticeships and on-the-job training, and developing a robust framework for recognition of prior learning (RPL).
• The rollout seeks to make education more holistic, practical, and employment-oriented, preparing students for the demands of the 21st-century job market.
• Special provisions are being made to ensure equitable access to vocational education for disadvantaged groups and in remote areas.

articles.trivia: The National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) was established in 2008 to promote skill development by catalyzing creation of large, quality, for-profit vocational institutions.

articles.headline: India-Japan Joint Naval Exercise 'JIMEX-26' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The 26th edition of the India-Japan Maritime Exercise (JIMEX-26) successfully concluded today in the Arabian Sea, marking a significant milestone in enhancing interoperability and cooperation between the two navies.
• The five-day exercise involved complex naval operations, including anti-submarine warfare, air defence drills, surface combat maneuvers, and advanced tactical exercises, demonstrating a high level of coordination.
• JIMEX-26 focused on strengthening maritime security in the Indo-Pacific region, addressing common challenges such as piracy, maritime terrorism, and ensuring freedom of navigation.
• Both navies exchanged best practices and refined their joint operational procedures, reinforcing their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based international order.
• The exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and Japan, particularly in the realm of defence and security cooperation.

articles.trivia: JIMEX started in 2012 as a bilateral maritime exercise between the Indian Navy and the Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF).

articles.headline: Lok Sabha Speaker Inaugurates 'Parliamentary Youth Summit' to Foster Civic Engagement
articles.explainer:
• The Lok Sabha Speaker today inaugurated the annual 'Parliamentary Youth Summit 2026' in New Delhi, bringing together young leaders from across the country to discuss pressing national issues and foster active civic engagement.
• The summit aims to educate youth about parliamentary procedures, the legislative process, and the importance of democratic participation in nation-building.
• Participants engaged in mock parliamentary debates, policy discussions, and interactive sessions with Members of Parliament and eminent personalities.
• Key themes for this year included 'Youth's Role in Achieving Vision Bharat 2047' and 'Leveraging Digital Platforms for Democratic Discourse'.
• The initiative underscores the commitment to nurturing future leaders and strengthening democratic values among the younger generation.

articles.trivia: India has the largest youth population in the world, with over 600 million people under the age of 25.

articles.headline: India's Wetland Conservation Efforts Gain Momentum: 10 New Ramsar Sites Identified
articles.explainer:
• In a major boost to biodiversity conservation, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today announced the identification of 10 new wetlands as Ramsar Sites, bringing India's total count to over 90.
• The newly added sites represent diverse ecosystems, including high-altitude wetlands, coastal lagoons, and freshwater marshes, spread across various states, further enhancing the network of protected wetlands.
• This expansion underscores India's commitment to the Ramsar Convention and its efforts to conserve ecologically sensitive wetland habitats, which are crucial for biodiversity, water security, and climate regulation.
• The declaration will facilitate enhanced conservation and management initiatives, including community participation, sustainable tourism, and research activities at these sites.
• Wetlands play a vital role in flood control, groundwater recharge, pollution abatement, and providing livelihoods to local communities.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.

articles.imageDescription: A serene wetland landscape with migratory birds, highlighting its ecological value.
articles.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha was designated as the first Ramsar Site of India in 1981.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Export Promotion Scheme for MSMEs in Niche Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) today launched a new 'MSME Export Accelerator Scheme' aimed at boosting exports from niche and emerging sectors, particularly those leveraging indigenous technologies and sustainable practices.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for market research, product certification, participation in international trade fairs, and development of export-oriented infrastructure.
• It specifically targets MSMEs in sectors such as organic products, traditional crafts with GI tags, advanced manufacturing components, and green technology solutions, encouraging them to tap into global markets.
• A dedicated portal and mentorship program will also be established to guide MSMEs through the complexities of international trade and compliance.
• The initiative is expected to significantly contribute to India's overall export growth and integrate MSMEs more deeply into global supply chains.

articles.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Gene Editing Technology for Agricultural Crop Improvement
articles.explainer:
• Indian scientists today announced a significant breakthrough in developing indigenous gene-editing technology, specifically tailored for enhancing the nutritional value and stress resistance of major agricultural crops.
• The research, conducted by a consortium of ICAR institutions and private biotech firms, has successfully demonstrated precise modifications in crops like rice and wheat to improve yield, drought tolerance, and pest resistance without introducing foreign DNA.
• This non-GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) approach, utilizing CRISPR-Cas9 derived tools, offers a promising path to sustainable agriculture and food security, addressing the challenges of climate change.
• The technology is expected to undergo further field trials and regulatory assessments, with potential for rapid deployment to benefit farmers and consumers.
• This achievement positions India as a leader in advanced agricultural biotechnology, reducing reliance on imported technologies.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.

articles.trivia: CRISPR-Cas9, often referred to as 'genetic scissors', was awarded the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2020.

articles.headline: India Pledges Significant Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at UN Forum
articles.explainer:
• At a special UN forum on Climate Finance today, India reiterated its commitment to climate justice by pledging substantial financial and technical support to the newly established 'Global South Climate Adaptation Fund'.
• The fund aims to assist developing nations, particularly Small Island Developing States (SIDS) and Least Developed Countries (LDCs), in building resilience against the adverse impacts of climate change.
• India emphasized the principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) and called for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments.
• The pledge highlights India's growing leadership in advocating for the interests of the Global South and fostering South-South cooperation on climate action.
• This initiative is expected to boost climate adaptation projects in vulnerable regions, focusing on areas like disaster preparedness, water management, and sustainable agriculture.

articles.headline: Election Commission Announces Schedule for Upcoming State Assembly Elections
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) today announced the detailed schedule for the upcoming Assembly Elections in three key states, setting the stage for a crucial political contest.
• The elections will be held in multiple phases, with polling dates spread across March and April, and results to be declared in early May.
• The ECI emphasized strict adherence to the Model Code of Conduct, ensuring a level playing field for all political parties and candidates.
• Special arrangements for voter awareness, accessibility for persons with disabilities, and technology-driven surveillance to prevent malpractices were highlighted.
• These elections are seen as a significant indicator of the political mood ahead of the next general elections, with major national and regional parties vying for power.

articles.headline: 'Poshan 2.0' Program Reviews Progress: Focus on Maternal and Child Nutrition Outcomes
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development today conducted a comprehensive review of the 'Poshan 2.0' program, assessing its progress in improving maternal and child nutrition outcomes across the country.
• The review highlighted significant strides in reducing stunting and wasting among children, and addressing anaemia among women and adolescent girls, attributing success to enhanced convergence of schemes and community participation.
• Focus areas for the next phase include strengthening the Anganwadi services, leveraging technology for real-time monitoring, and promoting dietary diversity and traditional food systems.
• Challenges such as regional disparities in nutritional status, last-mile delivery issues, and the impact of climate change on food security were also discussed.
• 'Poshan 2.0' aims for holistic nutrition and well-being, integrating various government initiatives under a unified framework.

articles.headline: Border Security Force Enhances Surveillance Technology Along Western Frontier
articles.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) today announced the deployment of advanced surveillance technology and integrated border management systems along critical stretches of India's western frontier.
• This initiative includes the installation of smart fences, long-range thermal imagers, ground surveillance radars, and drone-based monitoring systems to detect and deter infiltration attempts.
• The move aims to bolster border security, especially against cross-border terrorism, smuggling, and illegal migration, leveraging state-of-the-art indigenous technologies.
• BSF personnel are undergoing specialized training to operate and maintain these sophisticated systems, ensuring effective real-time intelligence gathering and response.
• The enhanced surveillance is part of a broader strategy to create an impregnable border, ensuring national security and territorial integrity.

articles.headline: RBI Expands Digital Rupee (CBDC) Pilot to Include More Banks and Geographies
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today announced the expansion of its pilot project for the Digital Rupee (e₹), India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), to include a wider range of public and private sector banks and additional geographical locations.
• This expansion aims to gather more comprehensive data on the operational efficiency, technological robustness, and public acceptance of both the wholesale (e₹-W) and retail (e₹-R) versions of the CBDC.
• The pilot will now involve more financial institutions and a larger user base, allowing for diverse use cases to be tested, including offline transactions and cross-border payments.
• The RBI reiterated its cautious and phased approach to CBDC implementation, focusing on ensuring financial stability and cybersecurity while leveraging the benefits of digital currency.
• The Digital Rupee is envisioned to complement existing forms of money, offering an additional payment avenue that is secure, efficient, and innovative.

Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

articles.headline: India-UK Free Trade Agreement: Significant Progress Reported in Latest Round of Talks
articles.explainer:
• The latest round of negotiations for the India-UK Free Trade Agreement (FTA) concluded today with both sides reporting significant progress on key contentious issues, raising hopes for an early conclusion.
• Discussions focused on market access for goods and services, intellectual property rights, rules of origin, and investor protection, with a renewed push to bridge remaining gaps.
• India is seeking greater access for its skilled professionals and agricultural products, while the UK is keen on reducing tariffs on its manufactured goods and spirits.
• The FTA is expected to boost bilateral trade and investment, creating new opportunities for businesses and strengthening economic ties between the two nations.
• Negotiators expressed optimism about reaching a mutually beneficial agreement that reflects the comprehensive strategic partnership.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Plant and Animal Species in Eastern Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian and international scientists today announced the discovery of several new species of plants and animals in the biodiversity-rich Eastern Himalayan region, highlighting the area's ecological significance.
• The discoveries include a new orchid species, a rare amphibian, and several insect species, adding to the immense biodiversity of one of the world's most critical biodiversity hotspots.
• These findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the Himalayas, which are facing threats from climate change, deforestation, and habitat loss.
• The research utilized advanced genomic sequencing and ecological survey techniques, providing valuable data for conservation planning and understanding evolutionary processes.
• The Eastern Himalayas are known for their unique flora and fauna, many of which are endemic and play crucial roles in maintaining ecosystem balance.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission Phase II Launched: Focus on Digital Governance and Citizen Services
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs today launched Phase II of the Smart Cities Mission, shifting focus towards advanced digital governance models, enhanced citizen services, and sustainable urban mobility.
• Building on the success of the first phase, Phase II aims to integrate AI and IoT technologies for smarter waste management, intelligent traffic systems, and real-time public service delivery in selected cities.
• A key objective is to empower urban local bodies with robust digital infrastructure and data analytics capabilities to improve urban planning and resource allocation.
• The new phase emphasizes citizen-centric approach, encouraging greater public participation in urban development and leveraging digital platforms for feedback and engagement.
• Sustainable urban development, including green buildings, urban forestry, and water conservation, remains a core component, ensuring livable and resilient cities.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Vision Bharat 2047: Green Growth & Digital Leap' blueprint, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve net-zero emissions by 2047.
2. It focuses on indigenous innovation in AI and quantum computing.
3. It emphasizes a circular economy model.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. India aims for Net-Zero emissions by 2070, while Vision Bharat 2047 outlines the strategic path towards sustainable development and technological leadership by 2047, not necessarily achieving net-zero by that specific year. Statements 2 and 3 are correct as the blueprint emphasizes fostering indigenous innovation in AI and quantum computing, and aims for a circular economy model.

prelimsMocks.question: The 'JIMEX-26' naval exercise, which recently concluded, is a joint exercise between India and which other country?
prelimsMocks.explanation: JIMEX stands for Japan-India Maritime Exercise. The 'JIMEX-26' naval exercise is a joint exercise between the Indian Navy and the Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF), focusing on enhancing interoperability and maritime cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'non-GMO approach' in the context of agricultural crop improvement, as mentioned in the recent breakthrough, primarily refers to which of the following techniques?

prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Utilizing CRISPR-Cas9 derived tools for precise gene modification without foreign DNA

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Begins Review of Electoral Bonds Scheme after Centre's Affidavit
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today commenced a crucial review of the Electoral Bonds Scheme, following a detailed affidavit submitted by the Union Government defending its transparency and constitutional validity.
• Petitioners have argued that the scheme promotes opacity in political funding and violates the 'right to know' of citizens, potentially leading to quid pro quo arrangements.
• The Centre's affidavit highlights the scheme's role in curbing black money in elections and providing a cleaner, digital channel for political donations, ensuring donor anonymity against harassment.
• The court is expected to hear arguments on various aspects, including the impact on free and fair elections, the balance between donor privacy and public transparency, and the implications for corporate funding.
• This review comes amidst ongoing public debate regarding electoral finance reforms and the need for greater accountability in political funding.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 'Sustainable Finance Working Group' Meeting in New Delhi
GS2.explainer:
• India today hosted the first 'Sustainable Finance Working Group' (SFWG) meeting of the G20 in New Delhi, focusing on mobilizing finance for climate action and sustainable development goals.
• Delegates from G20 member countries, invited guest countries, and international organizations participated, discussing strategies to enhance the flow of capital towards green and resilient infrastructure.
• Key agenda items included developing common taxonomies for sustainable finance, promoting blended finance mechanisms, and addressing debt sustainability challenges in developing nations.
• India's presidency emphasizes a 'human-centric' approach to global challenges, advocating for equitable access to climate finance and technology transfers.
• The outcomes of this meeting are expected to inform the broader G20 agenda on climate change and economic resilience for the year.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Focus on Vocational Training and Skill Development in Phase III Rollout
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education today announced the commencement of Phase III of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 implementation, with a significant emphasis on integrating vocational training and skill development across all levels of education.
• This phase aims to bridge the skill gap in the workforce by introducing vocational courses from middle school onwards, offering multiple entry and exit points, and ensuring industry alignment of curricula.
• Key initiatives include establishing more skill hubs, forging partnerships with industries for apprenticeships and on-the-job training, and developing a robust framework for recognition of prior learning (RPL).
• The rollout seeks to make education more holistic, practical, and employment-oriented, preparing students for the demands of the 21st-century job market.
• Special provisions are being made to ensure equitable access to vocational education for disadvantaged groups and in remote areas.

• The summit aims to educate youth about parliamentary procedures, the legislative process, and the importance of democratic participation in nation-building.
• Participants engaged in mock parliamentary debates, policy discussions, and interactive sessions with Members of Parliament and eminent personalities.
• Key themes for this year included 'Youth's Role in Achieving Vision Bharat 2047' and 'Leveraging Digital Platforms for Democratic Discourse'.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Significant Support for Global South Climate Adaptation Fund at UN Forum
GS2.explainer:
• At a special UN forum on Climate Finance today, India reiterated its commitment to climate justice by pledging substantial financial and technical support to the newly established 'Global South Climate Adaptation Fund'.
• The fund aims to assist developing nations, particularly Small Island Developing States (SIDS) and Least Developed Countries (LDCs), in building resilience against the adverse impacts of climate change.
• India emphasized the principle of 'Common but Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities' (CBDR-RC) and called for developed nations to fulfill their climate finance commitments.
• The pledge highlights India's growing leadership in advocating for the interests of the Global South and fostering South-South cooperation on climate action.
• This initiative is expected to boost climate adaptation projects in vulnerable regions, focusing on areas like disaster preparedness, water management, and sustainable agriculture.

GS2.headline: Election Commission Announces Schedule for Upcoming State Assembly Elections
GS2.explainer:

• The elections will be held in multiple phases, with polling dates spread across March and April, and results to be declared in early May.
• The ECI emphasized strict adherence to the Model Code of Conduct, ensuring a level playing field for all political parties and candidates.
• Special arrangements for voter awareness, accessibility for persons with disabilities, and technology-driven surveillance to prevent malpractices were highlighted.
• These elections are seen as a significant indicator of the political mood ahead of the next general elections, with major national and regional parties vying for power.

GS2.explainer:

• Focus areas for the next phase include strengthening the Anganwadi services, leveraging technology for real-time monitoring, and promoting dietary diversity and traditional food systems.
• Challenges such as regional disparities in nutritional status, last-mile delivery issues, and the impact of climate change on food security were also discussed.
• 'Poshan 2.0' aims for holistic nutrition and well-being, integrating various government initiatives under a unified framework.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047: Green Growth & Digital Leap' Blueprint
GS3.explainer:

• Key pillars include achieving net-zero emissions targets, expanding digital literacy and connectivity to every village, fostering indigenous innovation in AI and quantum computing, and positioning India as a global manufacturing hub for green technologies.
• The blueprint integrates economic growth with environmental protection, aiming for a circular economy model and significant improvements in resource efficiency across all sectors.
• It proposes a robust framework for public-private partnerships and international collaborations to mobilize the necessary capital and expertise for these transformative initiatives.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Advanced Test Flight of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Prototype
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of an advanced test flight of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator, marking a significant step towards cost-effective space access.
• The experimental flight involved a precise landing maneuver at an autonomous runway, demonstrating crucial capabilities for future orbital re-entry and landing of space vehicles.
• This test validates several critical technologies, including autonomous navigation, guidance and control, thermal protection systems, and re-entry trajectory management, essential for developing a fully reusable two-stage-to-orbit (TSTO) vehicle.
• ISRO aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making satellite deployment and human spaceflight more economically viable through RLV technology.
• The success reinforces India's position in the global space race and its commitment to indigenous technological advancements in space exploration.

Examine how this achievement aligns with India's broader vision for self-reliance (Atmanirbhar Bharat) in critical technologies.

GS3.trivia: ISRO first successfully tested the RLV-TD in a sub-orbital mission in 2016, demonstrating its hypersonic flight and re-entry capabilities.

GS3.headline: Revised National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) 2026-2030 Unveiled
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched the revised National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) for the period 2026-2030, aligning with India's enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• The updated plan incorporates new missions focusing on climate-resilient agriculture, urban sustainability, blue economy, and enhanced public participation in climate mitigation and adaptation efforts.
• It sets ambitious targets for increasing renewable energy capacity, improving energy efficiency, and promoting sustainable transportation and industrial practices.
• A key feature is the emphasis on leveraging digital technologies and data analytics for climate monitoring, early warning systems, and impact assessment across various sectors.
• The revised NAPCC aims to strengthen India's commitment to global climate goals while ensuring equitable and inclusive development domestically.

Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in leveraging digital technologies for effective climate monitoring and adaptation in India.
Examine the role of public participation and multi-stakeholder engagement in achieving the targets set by the revised NAPCC.
GS3.imageDescription: A visual representation of interconnected ecosystems and renewable energy sources, symbolizing the comprehensive nature of NAPCC.
GS3.trivia: The original NAPCC was launched in 2008, outlining eight national missions to address climate change.

GS3.headline: RBI Releases Half-Yearly Financial Stability Report: Highlights Resilient Banking Sector
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today released its latest half-yearly Financial Stability Report (FSR), indicating a resilient Indian banking sector amidst evolving global and domestic economic conditions.
• The report noted a continued improvement in asset quality for both public and private sector banks, with Non-Performing Assets (NPAs) showing a downward trend.
• It highlighted the robust capital adequacy ratios across the banking system, providing a strong buffer against potential shocks.
• However, the FSR cautioned about emerging risks from global economic slowdown, geopolitical tensions, and the increasing complexity of cybersecurity threats to the financial system.
• The report emphasized the need for continuous vigilance, prudent risk management, and further strengthening of regulatory frameworks to maintain financial stability.

GS3.headline: India-Japan Joint Naval Exercise 'JIMEX-26' Concludes, Enhancing Maritime Cooperation
GS3.explainer:
• The 26th edition of the India-Japan Maritime Exercise (JIMEX-26) successfully concluded today in the Arabian Sea, marking a significant milestone in enhancing interoperability and cooperation between the two navies.
• The five-day exercise involved complex naval operations, including anti-submarine warfare, air defence drills, surface combat maneuvers, and advanced tactical exercises, demonstrating a high level of coordination.
• JIMEX-26 focused on strengthening maritime security in the Indo-Pacific region, addressing common challenges such as piracy, maritime terrorism, and ensuring freedom of navigation.
• Both navies exchanged best practices and refined their joint operational procedures, reinforcing their commitment to a free, open, and rules-based international order.
• The exercise underscores the growing strategic partnership between India and Japan, particularly in the realm of defence and security cooperation.

GS3.headline: India's Wetland Conservation Efforts Gain Momentum: 10 New Ramsar Sites Identified
GS3.explainer:
• In a major boost to biodiversity conservation, the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today announced the identification of 10 new wetlands as Ramsar Sites, bringing India's total count to over 90.
• The newly added sites represent diverse ecosystems, including high-altitude wetlands, coastal lagoons, and freshwater marshes, spread across various states, further enhancing the network of protected wetlands.
• This expansion underscores India's commitment to the Ramsar Convention and its efforts to conserve ecologically sensitive wetland habitats, which are crucial for biodiversity, water security, and climate regulation.
• The declaration will facilitate enhanced conservation and management initiatives, including community participation, sustainable tourism, and research activities at these sites.
• Wetlands play a vital role in flood control, groundwater recharge, pollution abatement, and providing livelihoods to local communities.

GS3.trivia: Chilika Lake in Odisha was designated as the first Ramsar Site of India in 1981.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Export Promotion Scheme for MSMEs in Niche Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) today launched a new 'MSME Export Accelerator Scheme' aimed at boosting exports from niche and emerging sectors, particularly those leveraging indigenous technologies and sustainable practices.
• The scheme provides financial assistance for market research, product certification, participation in international trade fairs, and development of export-oriented infrastructure.
• It specifically targets MSMEs in sectors such as organic products, traditional crafts with GI tags, advanced manufacturing components, and green technology solutions, encouraging them to tap into global markets.
• A dedicated portal and mentorship program will also be established to guide MSMEs through the complexities of international trade and compliance.
• The initiative is expected to significantly contribute to India's overall export growth and integrate MSMEs more deeply into global supply chains.

GS3.headline: Breakthrough in Indigenous Gene Editing Technology for Agricultural Crop Improvement
GS3.explainer:
• Indian scientists today announced a significant breakthrough in developing indigenous gene-editing technology, specifically tailored for enhancing the nutritional value and stress resistance of major agricultural crops.
• The research, conducted by a consortium of ICAR institutions and private biotech firms, has successfully demonstrated precise modifications in crops like rice and wheat to improve yield, drought tolerance, and pest resistance without introducing foreign DNA.
• This non-GMO (Genetically Modified Organism) approach, utilizing CRISPR-Cas9 derived tools, offers a promising path to sustainable agriculture and food security, addressing the challenges of climate change.
• The technology is expected to undergo further field trials and regulatory assessments, with potential for rapid deployment to benefit farmers and consumers.
• This achievement positions India as a leader in advanced agricultural biotechnology, reducing reliance on imported technologies.

GS3.headline: Border Security Force Enhances Surveillance Technology Along Western Frontier
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Security Force (BSF) today announced the deployment of advanced surveillance technology and integrated border management systems along critical stretches of India's western frontier.
• This initiative includes the installation of smart fences, long-range thermal imagers, ground surveillance radars, and drone-based monitoring systems to detect and deter infiltration attempts.
• The move aims to bolster border security, especially against cross-border terrorism, smuggling, and illegal migration, leveraging state-of-the-art indigenous technologies.
• BSF personnel are undergoing specialized training to operate and maintain these sophisticated systems, ensuring effective real-time intelligence gathering and response.
• The enhanced surveillance is part of a broader strategy to create an impregnable border, ensuring national security and territorial integrity.

GS3.headline: RBI Expands Digital Rupee (CBDC) Pilot to Include More Banks and Geographies
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today announced the expansion of its pilot project for the Digital Rupee (e₹), India's Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC), to include a wider range of public and private sector banks and additional geographical locations.
• This expansion aims to gather more comprehensive data on the operational efficiency, technological robustness, and public acceptance of both the wholesale (e₹-W) and retail (e₹-R) versions of the CBDC.
• The pilot will now involve more financial institutions and a larger user base, allowing for diverse use cases to be tested, including offline transactions and cross-border payments.
• The RBI reiterated its cautious and phased approach to CBDC implementation, focusing on ensuring financial stability and cybersecurity while leveraging the benefits of digital currency.
• The Digital Rupee is envisioned to complement existing forms of money, offering an additional payment avenue that is secure, efficient, and innovative.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Plant and Animal Species in Eastern Himalayas
GS3.explainer:
• A team of Indian and international scientists today announced the discovery of several new species of plants and animals in the biodiversity-rich Eastern Himalayan region, highlighting the area's ecological significance.
• The discoveries include a new orchid species, a rare amphibian, and several insect species, adding to the immense biodiversity of one of the world's most critical biodiversity hotspots.
• These findings underscore the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts in the Himalayas, which are facing threats from climate change, deforestation, and habitat loss.
• The research utilized advanced genomic sequencing and ecological survey techniques, providing valuable data for conservation planning and understanding evolutionary processes.
• The Eastern Himalayas are known for their unique flora and fauna, many of which are endemic and play crucial roles in maintaining ecosystem balance.
